---
title: "assignment_"
author: "Aditi Dayal"
date: "2026-02-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.


---

# 1️⃣ Load Data
```{r load-data}
data <- read.csv("activity.csv")
head(data)
```


---

# 2️⃣ Plot 1 — Histogram of total steps per day
```{r plot1}
total_steps <- aggregate(steps ~ date, data, sum, na.rm = TRUE)

hist(total_steps$steps,
     main = "Total Steps per Day",
     xlab = "Total Steps",
     col = "lightblue")
```

---

# 3️⃣ Plot 2 — Average daily activity pattern
```r
```{r plot2}
interval_avg <- aggregate(steps ~ interval, data, mean, na.rm = TRUE)

plot(interval_avg$interval,
     interval_avg$steps,
     type = "l",
     xlab = "5-minute Interval",
     ylab = "Average Steps",
     main = "Average Daily Activity Pattern")
```
     

---

# 4️⃣ Handle Missing Values
```r
```{r impute}
data_filled <- data

for (i in 1:nrow(data_filled)) {
  if (is.na(data_filled$steps[i])) {
    data_filled$steps[i] <- interval_avg$steps[
      interval_avg$interval == data_filled$interval[i]
    ]
  }
}
```


---

# 5️⃣ Plot 3 — Histogram after filling missing values
```r
```{r plot3}
total_steps_filled <- aggregate(steps ~ date, data_filled, sum)

hist(total_steps_filled$steps,
     main = "Total Steps per Day (After Imputation)",
     xlab = "Total Steps",
     col = "lightgreen")
```
     

---

# 6️⃣ Plot 4 — Weekday vs Weekend activity pattern
```r
```{r plot4}
data_filled$date <- as.Date(data_filled$date)

data_filled$day_type <- ifelse(
  weekdays(data_filled$date) %in% c("Saturday","Sunday"),
  "weekend","weekday"
)

library(lattice)

interval_daytype <- aggregate(steps ~ interval + day_type,
                              data_filled,
                              mean)

xyplot(steps ~ interval | day_type,
       data = interval_daytype,
       type = "l",
       layout = c(1,2),
       xlab = "Interval",
       ylab = "Average Steps")
```
